creator: lbartoletti@FreeBSD.org
text:
Update GNU Solfege 3.20.2 -> 3.20.3

Fix: Shar :

# this line, and then unpack it by entering "sh file".  Note, it may
# create directories; files and directories will be owned by you and
# have default permissions.
#
# This archive contains:
#
#	solfege
#	solfege/Makefile
#	solfege/distinfo
#	solfege/pkg-descr
#	solfege/pkg-plist
#
echo c - solfege
mkdir -p solfege > /dev/null 2>&1
echo x - solfege/Makefile
sed 's/^X//' >solfege/Makefile << '7131d072bee3000e50fbf195c6bbe763'
X# ex:ts=8
X# Ports collection makefile for:	solfege
X# Date created:			Apr 10, 2003
X# Whom:				ijliao
X#
X# $FreeBSD: ports/audio/solfege/Makefile,v 1.59 2011/09/15 15:15:47 dhn Exp $
X#   $MCom: ports/audio/solfege/Makefile,v 1.7 2007/09/09 23:47:50 mezz Exp $
X#
X
XPORTNAME=	solfege
XPORTVERSION=	3.20.3
XCATEGORIES=	audio
XMASTER_SITES=	SF/${PORTNAME}/${PORTNAME}-stable/${PORTVERSION}
XPKGNAMESUFFIX=	-nosound
X
XMAINTAINER=	dhn@FreeBSD.org
XCOMMENT=	Free eartraining software
X
XBUILD_DEPENDS=	${STYLESHEET}:${PORTSDIR}/textproc/docbook-xsl \
X		txt2man:${PORTSDIR}/textproc/txt2man 
X
XUSE_GNOME=	gnomehack pygtk2
XWANT_GNOME=	yes
XUSE_PYTHON=	yes
XUSE_GETTEXT=	yes
XUSE_GMAKE=	yes
XGNU_CONFIGURE=	yes
XCONFIGURE_ENV=	ac_cv_path_XML2PO=""
XCONFIGURE_ARGS=	--disable-oss-sound \
X		--enable-docbook-stylesheet=${STYLESHEET}
XMAKE_ARGS=	skipmanual=yes
XUSE_DISPLAY=	yes
X
XMAN1=		solfege.1
X
XSTYLESHEET=	${LOCALBASE}/share/xsl/docbook/xhtml/chunk.xsl
X
X# Restrict to stable (even) versions, indicated by the second component.
XPORTSCOUT=	limitw:1,even
X
X.include <bsd.port.pre.mk>
X
X.if ${HAVE_GNOME:Mpygnomeextras}!=""
XUSE_GNOME+=	pygnomeextras
X.endif
X
Xpost-patch:
X	@${GREP} -lR "/usr/bin/python" ${WRKSRC} | ${XARGS} \
X		${REINPLACE_CMD} -e 's|/usr/bin/python|${SETENV} python|g'
X	@${REINPLACE_CMD} -e 's|type=sequencer-device|type=fake-synth|g ; \
X		 s|/usr/bin|${LOCALBASE}/bin|g' ${WRKSRC}/default.config
X	@${REINPLACE_CMD} -e 's|(datadir)/locale|(prefix)/share/locale|g' \
X		${WRKSRC}/po/Makefile
X	@${REINPLACE_CMD} -i '' -e 's|/bin/bash|/bin/sh|g' \
X		${WRKSRC}/exercises/standard/lesson-files/bin/csound-play-harmonic-interval.sh
X
X.include <bsd.port.post.mk>

XWhen you study music on high school, college or music conservatory, you
Xusually have to do ear training. Some of the exercises, like sight singing
Xis easy to do alone. But often you have to be at least two people, one
Xmaking questions, the other answering.
X
XGNU Solfege tries to help out with this. Solfege is a computer program written
Xto help you practise the more simple and mechanical exercises on your own.
XThese are the exercises written so far:
X   * Recognise melodic and harmonic intervals
X   * Compare interval sizes
X   * Sing the intervals the computer asks for
X   * Identify chords
X   * Sing chords
X   * Scales
X   * Dictation
X   * Remembering rhythmic patterns
X
XWWW: http://www.solfege.org/
19f074ac8eed53414029b3fd5fd6032e
